one_line_thesis: Weyerhaeuser is the largest private U.S. timberland REIT (10M+ acres, integrated mills). At ~$23.51, normalized Adjusted FAD ~$0.65/sh yields -4.84% synthesis IRR; watch at lumber trough despite land floor.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Weyerhaeuser is the largest private U.S. timberland owner: 10 million+ acres, integrated mills, and a REIT cash-return framework targeting 75-80% of Adjusted FAD through a $0.72 base dividend plus buybacks and supplemental dividends. FY2025 Adjusted FAD was $397 million (~$0.55 per share) at a lumber trough; we normalize owner cash to ~$0.65 per share for the base case. Leverage rose to $5.57 billion long-term debt in 2025 after term-loan refinancing. At ~$23.51, our base case annual return is -4.84% per year on normalized Adjusted FAD (7-year total synthesis). Book equity and acreage provi...

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Scarce assets, royalties, and optionality
applicability_tests: The security owns a legally enforceable economic claim on scarce assets or royalties.
Material value is omitted or poorly represented by current GAAP earnings.
Asset quantities, ownership, overlap, timing, and remaining capital can be reconciled.
Comparable values can be adjusted for location, quality, control, liquidity, and realization time.
failure_modes: gross asset value mistaken for shareholder value
operating cash flow and NAV counted twice
remote options treated as contracted cash

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

bullets.title: Net income down 18%
bullets.summary: Net income moved -18.2% versus the prior period (396.0 to 324.0).

timeline.label: Net income moved -18.2% versus the prior period (396.0 to 324.0).
Sold 108,000 Virginia timberland acres for $192M; $58M gain recorded in Q1 2026 Timberlands segment
Issued $102M resource recovery revenue bonds; part of $496M net long-term debt increase in 2025
Issued $800M senior unsecured term loan; repaid ~$500M of 4.75% senior notes; entered $800M interest rate swaps
Amended revolving credit facility to $1.75B, extended maturity to June 2030
Repaid $139M 8.50% debentures and $71M 7.95% debentures; launched $1B share repurchase program
Announced ~$500M Monticello, Arkansas TimberStrand engineered wood products facility; construction began 2025, ops target 2027
Paid supplemental dividend $0.14/sh ($102M total) based on 2023 results under flexible cash return framework
Adopted flexible cash return framework targeting 75-80% of annual Adjusted FAD via base dividend plus buybacks/supplemental dividend
Converted to real estate investment trust structure; timber REIT tax status affects dividend and capital allocation
Portfolio simplification: exited International Timberlands; focused on North American integrated timber and wood products platform
Lumber super-cycle peak: Wood Products Adjusted EBITDA reached multi-year highs before 2022-2025 normalization
Merged Weyerhaeuser with Plum Creek Timber; created largest private U.S. timberland owner (~13M acres at close)

industry.structure: U.S. timber REITs are an oligopoly of large acreage owners (WY, RYN post-merger, others) integrated with sawmills and engineered wood products. Scale, geographic diversification, and internal fiber supply create barriers; log and lumber prices remain cyclical and commodity-exposed.
industry.share_shift: Rayonier-PotlatchDeltic merger (Jan 2026) created a larger pure-play competitor at ~4.1M acres; WY retains scale lead at 10M+ U.S. acres with deeper mill integration. Southern timber harvest competition intensifies as institutional capital consolidates acreage.
industry.trend: Lumber markets remain in post-2022 trough; housing starts and repair/remodel demand drive Wood Products recovery timing. Carbon solutions, renewable energy leases, and HBU land sales add non-timber monetization. Interest rates and leverage matter for REIT capital return.
